prs.title: ## Reusable Empty State Component for Consistent UI (#9799)
prs.body: ## Overview Currently, multiple pages across the frontend implement their own custom empty states. This duplicates markup and results in inconsistent UI styles and typography across the platform. This PR introduces a reusable `EmptyState.vue` component to standardize empty states across the codebase. We have migrated **6 key list views** to use this component, unified all localization strings, and verified the changes through full test execution. ### Fixes #9799 --- ## Proposed Solution #### 1. Reusable `EmptyState.vue` Component Created a self-contained component at `frontend/www/js/omegaup/components/common/EmptyState.vue` with the following configuration: * **Props**: * `icon` (string | string[]): FontAwesome icon to display. * `title` (string): Central header text. * `description` (string): Smaller contextual explanation. * `buttonText` (string, optional): Text for the primary action button. * `buttonLink` (string, optional): Path target for the primary action button. * **Flexibility**: * Emits an `'action'` event if a action button is clicked without a `buttonLink`. * Support for an `#action` slot for advanced, customized action buttons. * Automatically bundles common FontAwesome icons (`users`, `graduation-cap`, `clipboard-list`, `terminal`). --- ### Components Migrated | View Name | Component File | Empty State Context | | :--- | :--- | :--- | | **Teams Groups** | `teamsgroup/List.vue` | Replaced duplicate inline structure. | | **Manage Courses** | `course/Mine.vue` | Custom message and new course button. | | **Manage Contests** | `contest/Mine.vue` | Custom message and new contest button. | | **Manage Problems** | `problem/Mine.vue` | Custom message and new problem button. | | **Global Runs** | `arena/Runs.vue` | Dynamic run state table list inside ``. | | **Quality Nominations** | `qualitynomination/List.vue` | Report list tracking status. | --- ### Internationalization (i18n) Standardized and synced new translation keys across English, Spanish, Portuguese, and Pseudolocale (`.lang`, `.json`, and `.ts` structures): * `problemListEmptyTitle` / `problemListEmptyDescription` * `runsListEmptyTitle` / `runsListEmptyDescription` * `qualityNominationListEmptyTitle` / `qualityNominationListEmptyDescription` * `contestListEmptyTitle` / `contestListEmptyDescription` --- ### Testing & Verification 1. **Unit Tests**: * Updated `teamsgroup/List.test.ts` to change `shallowMount` to `mount`. * **Why this change?** `shallowMount` stubs child components, which prevents the parent test wrapper from seeing and asserting on the text nested inside our new `` child component. Using `mount` does a full recursive render of descendants so assertions can verify text output correctly. * Ran complete test suite successfully: ```bash npm run test # Output: 183 passed, 183 total test suites (838 tests passed) ``` 2. **Webpack Build**: * Recompiled all assets successfully with zero errors. ### Screenshots

prs.title: Proof of Concept (PoC) scoped to Issue #9847,  fields explicitly defined as `NOT NULL` in the MySQL schema are generated as nullable properties

labels.name: php
labels.description: Pull requests that update Php code

labels.name: python
labels.description: Pull requests that update Python code

prs.body:
# Description

This PR is a Proof of Concept (PoC) scoped to Issue #9847 focussing on the `Team_Groups` table to address the mismatch where fields explicitly defined as `NOT NULL` in the MySQL schema are generated as nullable properties (e.g., `string|null` or optional properties) in the auto-generated VO models.


Solving this at the generator level removes the need for defensive coding, manual type casting (`strval()`, `intval()`), and redundant assertions across both backend controllers (Psalm) and frontend Vue templates (TypeScript).

***

### The Architecture: Generic and Maintainable
Rather than hardcoding table-specific rules inside the template, this PR establishes a **fully generic, schema-respecting code generator**. 

A transition whitelist is introduced at the top of `stuff/dao_utils.py`:
```python
_STRICT_TYPES_TABLES = {
    'Team_Groups',
}
```

Any table added to this whitelist is dynamically compiled using strictly-typed modern PHP properties (e.g. `public string $name = '';`) with sensible, type-safe default initializations. This keeps all other legacy tables completely untouched, preventing code churn while enabling a highly controlled, table-by-table rollout.


---


## Proof of Improvement & Verification

To verify that the rules are solid and correct, we successfully ran a full system check:

1. **Psalm (Static Analysis)**
   * **Verification Command:** `docker exec omegaup-frontend-1 ./vendor/bin/psalm`
   * **Result:** **No errors found!** All controller updates compile cleanly under strict Psalm checks.
   
2. **Frontend Compilation**
   * **Verification Command:** `npm run build`
   * **Result:** **Compiled successfully!** Webpack and TypeScript compilation finished flawlessly, correctly accepting the updated, strict `api_types.ts` parameters without optional `?` markers.

---

## Detailed Changes

### 1. Code Generator & Templates
* **`stuff/dao_utils.py`**: Added the `_STRICT_TYPES_TABLES` whitelist. Enabled `Table` objects to dynamically parse and enforce correct non-nullable `php_type` configurations for `NOT NULL` columns when a table is whitelisted.
* **`stuff/dao_templates/vo.php`**: Integrated generic support for PHP 8.1 strictly-typed property signatures (e.g. `public int $team_group_id`). Added safe, type-specific default fallback initializations (e.g. `''`, `0`, `false`) for non-nullable properties that do not have database-level defaults.
* **`frontend/cmd/APITool.php`**: Updated the properties generator loop in `generateTypes()` to verify Psalm's `returnType->isNullable()`. It now outputs strict property interfaces (e.g., `alias: string;`) for non-nullable database columns and only prints the optional `?` marker for nullable columns.



### 2. Auto-Generated & Regenerated Files
* **`frontend/server/src/DAO/VO/TeamGroups.php`**: Upgraded properties to strict modern PHP 8.1 typed declarations with sensible defaults.
* **`frontend/server/src/DAO/Base/TeamGroups.php`**: Upgraded base methods (e.g., `getByPK`) to enforce correct strict parameter bindings and simplified query binding arrays.
* **`frontend/www/js/omegaup/api_types.ts`**: Correctly generated TypeScript interfaces for `TeamGroups` and other whitelisted fields with accurate non-optional properties.


### 3. Controller Cleanup (Debt Removed)
Cleaned up several manual casting workarounds and redundant check assertions:
* **`frontend/server/src/Controllers/Contest.php`**: Removed redundant `!is_null` check on `teamsGroup->name`.
* **`frontend/server/src/Controllers/Identity.php`**: Removed redundant `is_null` checks on `teamGroup->alias`.
* **`frontend/server/src/Controllers/TeamsGroup.php`**: Cast `$teamGroupAcl->acl_id` to `intval` to safely assign to strictly typed `$teamGroup->acl_id`, and removed redundant `is_null` checks on `teamsGroup->team_group_id`.

---

## Plan of Action (Next Steps)

Now that this PoC has validated the entire pipeline (from schema parsing to PHP static analysis and Webpack compilation), then upon @pabo99 your validation and feedback, the rollout plan is as follows:

1. **Deploy and Monitor:** Merge this PoC to validate the runtime behaviors and gather community feedback on the generated types.
2. **Phase 2 Migration:** Identify the next high-traffic tables (e.g., `Users`, `Contests`, or `Problems`) and add them to `_STRICT_TYPES_TABLES`.
3. **Incremental Cleanups:** Fix the corresponding Psalm errors in their controllers by removing redundant null checks/castings.
4. **Generalization:** Once all high-priority tables are successfully whitelisted, make strict-typing the default state for all tables, completely retiring the legacy generation fallback and whitelist.
```

prs.title: Update README.md
chore: add .sqlfluff configuration file for SQL linting
prs.body:
***

## Description:

### Problem
Currently, SQL formatting is not strictly enforced in our automated tests. This can lead to inconsistent SQL styles (mixed casing, spacing issues) and requires manual effort during code reviews to catch formatting discrepancies. 

### Solution
This PR introduces a `.sqlfluff` configuration file to lay the groundwork for automated SQL linting. [SQLFluff](https://docs.sqlfluff.com/) is an industry-standard, dialect-specific SQL linter.

***

**Note:** This PR *only* adds the configuration file and does not modify any existing `.sql` files or workflow actions yet. The intention is to establish the ruleset first, with the ultimate goal of adding a GitHub Action step later that only lints *modified* `.sql` files in future PRs (preventing unnecessary noise on the legacy codebase).


### Configuration Details
I have tested this configuration locally against the existing `frontend/database/*.sql` files to ensure it respects omegaUp's current style conventions rather than fighting them:

* **Dialect:** Set to `mysql`.
* **Indentation:** Set to `2` spaces to match existing database migration files.
* **Line Length:** Increased to `120` to accommodate standard queries and avoid overly aggressive wrapping warnings.
* **Excluded Rules:**
  * `RF06`: Excluded because omegaUp extensively uses backticks (`` ` ``) for table and column names.
  * `LT02` & `LT09`: Excluded because these layout rules are too pedantic regarding indentation inside DDL statements (like `ALTER TABLE`) and would create unnecessary noise.
  
  ---

### Related Issue
#9848 

### Testing Locally
If you have Python installed, you can test this configuration locally on any SQL file:
```bash
pip install sqlfluff
sqlfluff lint frontend/database/00002_timezone.sql
```

***

prs.body:
## Motivation

This PR completes the implementation of the soft-delete (archiving) mechanism for Team Groups. Following the successful deployment of the database schema in Part 1, this PR propagates those changes through the DAO, Controller, and API layers.

## Implemented Changes

### 1. DAO Regeneration

- Regenerated `VO/TeamGroups.php` and `Base/TeamGroups.php` using the official DAO generator  
- Included support for the `archived` field  
- Preserved localized Spanish comments in generated docblocks  

### 2. Controller Support

#### Update API
- Modified `TeamsGroup::apiUpdate` to allow administrators to toggle the `archived` status of a team group  

#### Details API
- Updated `apiDetails` and the TypeScript payload generator to return the `archived` status  

#### Administrative Queries
- Updated `TeamGroups::getAllTeamsGroupsAdminedByUser` to fetch the `archived` column for proper display in management views  

### 3. TypeScript Integration

Updated `api_types.ts` to include:

```ts
archived: boolean
```

in the relevant interfaces:

- `TeamsGroup`
- `TeamsGroupDetailsResponse`
- `TeamGroupEditPayload`

### 4. Documentation

- Updated the API `README.md` to reflect the new `archived` field in request/response payloads  


### Database Sync

- Verified development and test databases are synchronized with the updated schema  

## Checklist

- [x] DAO regenerated successfully  
- [x] Controller logic updated  
- [x] API payloads updated  
- [x] TypeScript types synchronized  
- [x] Documentation updated  
- [x] Tests passing  
- [x] Linting passing  
- [x] Ready for review  

 Fixes #9828 

Part1- #9832

prs.title: [Part 1/2] Add `archived` field to `Team_Groups` (Schema Migration)
prs.body:
# [Part 1/2] Add `archived` field to `Team_Groups` (Schema Migration)

## Motivation
This is the first part of the implementation for Team Group archiving (soft delete). To ensure a safe deployment and allow for easy rollbacks, this PR includes **only** the database schema changes and the migration script. 

The second part (DAO regeneration and Controller updates) is drafted and will be rechecked and pushed once this schema change is verified. https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/pull/9831

## Implemented Changes
- **Migration Script**: Created `frontend/database/00271_add_team_groups_archived_field.sql` to add the `archived` column (`TINYINT(1) DEFAULT 0`) to the `Team_Groups` table.
- **Schema Update**: Updated `frontend/database/schema.sql` to reflect the new table structure.

## Deployment Plan
1.  **Step 1 (This PR)**: Deploy the schema migration to production.
2.  **Verification**: Confirm the database has been updated correctly.
3.  **Step 2 (Follow-up)**: Run the DAO generator and update the API Controller logic to utilize the new field.

### Fixes https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/issues/9828

***

prs.title: Add `archived` field for Team Groups (Soft Delete Support) (Backend)

labels.name: php
labels.description: Pull requests that update Php code

prs.body:
## Motivation

Currently, team groups cannot be removed or hidden, leading to the accumulation of temporary, duplicate, or outdated groups. This makes management difficult for organizers and clutters the UI.

This PR introduces a soft-delete (archive) mechanism by adding an `archived` field to the `Team_Groups` table. This allows groups to be marked as inactive while preserving referential integrity for existing data.

## Proposed Solution

Following the design pattern used in other core tables (e.g., `Courses`, `Contests`), an `archived` column has been introduced along with full backend support.

---

## Implemented Changes

### 1. Database Schema

- **Migration**: Added `frontend/database/00271_add_team_groups_archived_field.sql` to introduce the `archived` column  
- **Schema**: Updated `frontend/database/schema.sql` to include the `archived` column in the `Team_Groups` table definition  

### 2. DAO Layer

- **VO (Value Object)**: Updated `TeamGroups` VO to include the `archived` property and map it in the constructor  
- **Base DAO**: Updated to include `archived` in `create`, `update`, `getByPK`, and `getAll` methods  
- **Main DAO**: Updated custom queries (e.g., `getAllTeamsGroupsAdminedByUser`) to fetch the `archived` field  

### 3. API & Controller Support

- **`TeamsGroup::apiUpdate`**: Extended to accept an optional `archived` boolean parameter  
- **`TeamsGroup::apiDetails`**: Included `archived` in the response  
- **Edit Payload**: Added `archived` to `getTeamGroupEditDetailsForTypeScript`  
- **Type Safety**: Updated Psalm type definitions across the controller  

---

## Design Considerations

- **Naming Consistency**: Used `archived` to match conventions in other tables (`Courses`, `Contests`, `Problems`)  
- **Backward Compatibility**: Defaults to `0` (active), ensuring no disruption  
- **Non-Breaking API**: `apiUpdate` remains backward compatible if `archived` is omitted  

## Testing

- Verified creation, update, and retrieval of team groups with `archived` field  
- Confirmed backward compatibility with existing workflows  
- No regressions observed  

---

### Additional Context

Frontend - https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/pull/9798 , will finish that once approval of the backend changes

## Checklist

- [x] Migration added and tested  
- [x] Schema updated  
- [x] DAO layer updated  
- [x] API updated (non-breaking)  
- [x] Type definitions updated  
- [x] Tested locally  
- [x] Ready for review  


## Fixes - https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/issues/9833

prs.title: Refactor `$once('hook:beforeDestroy')` usage to `beforeDestroy` lifecycle hook
prs.body:
## Description

This PR replaces the usage of `$once('hook:beforeDestroy', ...)` with the standard Vue `beforeDestroy` lifecycle hook to simplify cleanup logic and improve maintainability.

## Changes

- Replaced `$once('hook:beforeDestroy', ...)` with `beforeDestroy`
- Ensured event listeners are properly removed during component teardown
- Maintained existing functionality without regressions

## Affected Files

- `omegaup/frontend/www/js/omegaup/group/edit.ts`
- `omegaup/frontend/www/js/omegaup/teamsgroup/edit.ts`

---

## Before

```ts
groupEdit.$once('hook:beforeDestroy', () => {
  window.removeEventListener('hashchange', onHashChange);
});
```


## After

```ts
beforeDestroy() {
  window.removeEventListener('hashchange', onHashChange);
},
```
---

## Benefits

- Improves code clarity and readability  
- Aligns with Vue lifecycle conventions  
- Easier to understand and maintain  
- Consistent with existing patterns (e.g., `contest_listv2.ts`)  

## Testing

- Verified event listeners are cleaned up on component destroy  
- No functional regressions observed  
- Existing tests pass  

---

## Checklist

- [x] Code follows project conventions  
- [x] No breaking changes  
- [x] Tested locally  
- [x] Ready for review  

### Fixes https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/issues/9829

Feature: Archive (Soft Delete) for Team Groups

labels.name: php
labels.description: Pull requests that update Php code

prs.body: #9726 ### Motivation Currently, team groups cannot be removed or hidden. Over time, this leads to accumulation of: - temporary/test groups - duplicate groups - outdated groups This creates UI clutter and makes it harder for organizers to manage team groups effectively. --- ## Current Progress (This PR) This PR focuses on **frontend groundwork and UX exploration**, without introducing any backend or schema changes yet. ### Changes implemented: - Added UI structure in `teamsgroup/List.vue` to support: - Filtering of team groups (designed to exclude archived ones) - Placeholder "Archive" action (non-persistent for now) - Refactored list rendering to support derived/computed filtering logic - Ensured existing behavior remains unchanged (no breaking changes) ### Why this approach: - Keeps the PR **safe and reviewable** - Avoids premature backend changes before confirming direction - Allows validation of UX and interaction patterns first --- ## Proposed Solution (Planned) Introduce an **archive (soft delete)** mechanism using an `is_archived` flag. ### Expected behavior: - Archived team groups are **hidden from the default list view** - They are **not selectable in new workflows** (e.g., contest creation) - They **remain in the database** to preserve referential integrity - Existing contests using archived groups continue to function normally --- ### Planned Implementation (Next Steps) ### 1. Database Add a new column: ```sql is_archived TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 ``` ### 2. Backend (API) - Extend `TeamsGroup::apiUpdate` to accept optional `is_archived` - Update the model conditionally (non-breaking) - Include `is_archived` in API responses > Note: Initial plan is to avoid API-level filtering to prevent breaking existing flows/tests. Filtering would be handled in the frontend first. --- ### 3. Frontend (Follow-up) - Finalize archive action with API integration - Hide archived groups by default - Add optional "Show archived" toggle - Prevent archived groups from appearing in selection dropdowns --- ### Design Considerations - **Backward compatibility**: Existing contests referencing team groups must continue to work - **Data safety**: Avoid hard deletion to prevent breaking references - **Incremental rollout**: Validate UX and API contract before introducing global filtering - **Consistency**: Ensure archived groups behave predictably across all UI surfaces --- ### Questions / Feedback Requested 1. Is introducing an `is_archived` flag the preferred approach for this use case? 2. Should filtering of archived groups eventually happen at the **API level**, or remain frontend-driven? 3. Are there existing patterns in omegaUp for soft deletion or archiving that should be followed? 4. Would restore/unarchive functionality be expected in scope, or should this remain one-way initially? 5. Are there any edge cases (e.g., contests, scoreboards, permissions) that should be explicitly handled? --- Screenshot of draft UI Also plan to alter the colour of used archive icon from fontsawesome to rgb(197, 215, 234) to keep it consistent with the theme of OmegaUp

prs.title: Add controller support for social media fields in user profile

labels.name: php
labels.description: Pull requests that update Php code

prs.body:
## Description

This PR implements controller-level support for the social media fields (`x_url`, `linkedin_url`, `github_url`) as part of the feature to add social media links to user profiles.

---

### Changes

* Added handling of `x_url`, `linkedin_url`, and `github_url` in `apiUpdate`
* Added validation using existing `Validators::isValidURL`
* Supported partial updates using `hasParameter` to avoid unintended overwrites
* Supported deletion of fields by allowing `null` values
* Included social media fields in `getProfileImpl` response

---

### Design Considerations

* All fields are optional to maintain backward compatibility
* Validation leverages existing utilities to follow codebase patterns
* Updates are applied only when parameters are explicitly provided
* No changes were made to existing workflows or unrelated fields

---

### Dependencies

* Depends on DAO updates for social media fields (#8860)
* Part of #8692 (Add Social Media Links to User Profile)

---

### Notes

* Existing functionality remains unaffected
* API responses now include social media fields when available

---

Fixes #8867

prs.title: Restore browser history & URL hash synchronization for sublinks in Contest/edit
prs.body:
##  Fix: Restore browser history & URL hash synchronization in Arena and Activity pages

### Description

This PR fixes inconsistent browser navigation behavior caused by missing synchronization between UI state and URL hash in both:

* `/arena/<alias>/`
* `/contest/<alias>/activity/`

Previously, tab navigation in these pages relied partially or entirely on internal component state, leading to broken browser history behavior.

---

##  Problems

### 1. Submissions (Arena page)

* Used `history.replaceState` for tab navigation
* Overwrote history entries instead of creating new ones
* Back button skipped intermediate tabs (`runs → ranking → clarifications`)

### 2. Activity report(Activity page)

* Tab navigation used only local state (`showTab`)
* URL hash was never updated
* `hashchange` events were never triggered
* Back/forward navigation did not work

---

##  Root Cause

Across both pages, there was a common architectural issue:

> UI state (active tab) was not synchronized with URL state (hash)

Specifically:

* Arena used `replaceState`, breaking history stack
* Activity did not use URL at all
* Components did not react to URL changes

---

## Changes

### Submissions (Arena)

* Replaced `history.replaceState` with `window.location.hash`
* Introduced reactive `activeTab` in parent Vue instance
* Synced hash → state via `hashchange` listener
* Fixed child component desync in `<omegaup-arena>` by syncing `selectedTab` with `activeTab`:

```ts
@Watch('activeTab')
onActiveTabChanged(newTab: string) {
  this.selectedTab = newTab;
}
```

---

### Activity Feed

* Replaced direct state mutation (`showTab = ...`) with URL-driven navigation:

```ts
onTabChange(tab: string) {
  if (this.showTab !== tab) {
    this.showTab = tab;
    window.location.hash = tab;
  }
}
```

* Added URL → UI synchronization:

```ts
mounted() {
  window.addEventListener('hashchange', this.syncFromHash);
  this.syncFromHash();
}

syncFromHash() {
  const tab = window.location.hash.replace('#', '') || 'report';
  if (this.showTab !== tab) {
    this.showTab = tab;
  }
}
```

---

##  Result

### Arena

* Browser back/forward navigation correctly traverses:

  ```
  #clarifications → #ranking → #runs → edit page
  ```

### Activity

* Tabs now update URL:

  ```
  #report → #users → #origins
  ```
* Back/forward navigation works as expected

---

###


 Scope & Depth

While the visible UI change is small, the fix addresses deeper architectural issues:

* Improper use of History API (`replaceState`)
* Missing URL-state integration
* Lack of reactivity across component boundaries
* Absence of `hashchange` handling

This PR introduces a consistent pattern:

> URL hash is the single source of truth for tab navigation

---

## Testing

* Verified Arena tab navigation and back/forward behavior
* Verified Activity tab navigation and back/forward behavior
* Confirmed no regressions in rendering or data flow
* No console errors observed

Screen-Recordings

Before 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b4e545d8-c569-45ec-89b8-b487229e8f55

After

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/16c0c556-bada-469c-8bcd-bc25c259112c

---

Fixes #9660

prs.title: #9676 Improve empty state for Teams Groups page
prs.body:
## 🧩 Improve empty state for Teams Groups page 
Fixes: #9676 

### Description
Currently, when no teams groups have been created, the interface displays an empty table. This can be confusing for users and does not provide clear guidance on what to do next.

This PR introduces a user-friendly empty state for the Teams Groups page, improving usability and maintaining consistency with other parts of the platform (e.g., course list page).

---

### Changes implemented
- Added a computed property (`hasTeamsGroups`) to detect when no teams groups exist
- Replaced the empty table with a centered empty state UI including:
  - FontAwesome icon (`users`)
  - Title and description
  - Prominent "Create new teams group" button
- Preserved existing table layout when teams groups are present
- Added translations for:
  - en
  - es
  - pt
  - pseudo
- Followed the same empty state pattern used in `course/mine`

---

###  Impact
- Improves user experience by guiding users when no data is present
- Reduces confusion for first-time users
- Ensures UI consistency across the platform

---

### Checklist
- [x] UI updated with empty state
- [x] Translations added for all languages
- [x] Tests updated/added
- [x] No lint or type errors

prs.title: Fix: Back button tab sync issue in group and teamsgroup edit pages
prs.body:
## Summary
This PR fixes an issue where the browser back/forward navigation did not correctly update the active tab in:
Fixes: https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/pull/9549

- `group/edit`
- `teamsgroup/edit`

## Problem
When navigating between tabs (Edit / Members / Teams / Upload) and using the browser back button:
- The URL hash (`#edit`, `#teams`, etc.) changed
- But the UI tab (`selectedTab`) did not update accordingly

This caused inconsistency between the URL and the displayed content.

## Root Cause
The Vue component state (`selectedTab`) was not properly synchronized with:
- The `tab` prop from parent
- The browser `window.location.hash`

## Solution
- Synced `tab` (prop) → `selectedTab` using Vue `@Watch`
- Synced `selectedTab` → parent via `$emit('update:tab')`
- Ensured correct initialization of tab state on mount
- Ensured hash changes properly reflect in UI state

## Result
- Browser back/forward navigation works correctly
- URL hash and UI tab remain in sync
- Consistent behavior across both `group` and `teamsgroup` modules

## Consistency
This follows the same pattern used in previous fixes (e.g., #9445), and applies it consistently across similar pages.

---

## Checklist

- [x] Fix applied to `group/edit`
- [x] Fix applied to `teamsgroup/edit`
- [x] URL hash and UI tab stay in sync
- [x] Browser back/forward navigation works correctly
- [x] No regression in tab switching behavior
- [x] Code follows existing patterns in codebase
- [x] Manually tested locally

---

##  Testing

### Manual Testing
- Navigated between tabs (Edit / Teams / Upload)
- Used browser back and forward buttons
- Verified correct tab is shown based on URL

---

## Before / After 
Before:
- URL changed but tab UI did not update

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d1cc7f17-a4d3-474d-bfe3-2a752179ee9b



After:
- URL and tab UI remain fully synchronized


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/beb90231-cfa0-42b7-8e9d-d7d5fd1c2a26


---

## 🙌 Notes
Happy to split into separate PRs if preferred, but since this is the same issue across similar modules, keeping it unified for consistency.

prs.title: Fix: Browser Back/Forward buttons do not update active tab on Edit Course page
prs.body:
## Description

Fixes an issue where using the browser Back/Forward buttons on the **Edit Course** page updates the URL hash but does not update the visible tab.

The component initializes the tab state from `initialTab`, and tab changes triggered via click handlers correctly update `showTab`. However, when navigating using the browser history (Back/Forward), only the URL hash changes while the component state remains unchanged, causing the UI to stay on the last viewed tab.

## Changes
- Added a `hashchange` event listener to synchronize `showTab` with the URL hash when the user navigates using browser Back/Forward buttons.
- Initialized the active tab from the URL hash when the component is mounted.
- Removed the event listener in `beforeDestroy` to prevent potential memory leaks.

## Additional Fix
While implementing the fix, it was noticed that the `archive` tab exists in the template but was missing from the `availableTabs` list. This prevented proper validation when syncing the tab state with the URL hash. The `archive` tab has been added to `availableTabs` to ensure consistent behavior.

## Reference
This follows the same pattern implemented in #9396 for handling hash-based tab navigation.

Fixes #9445

## Screen Recording of Fix

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d1e803c1-6125-479e-8794-cb42197ded2d



## Checklist

- [x] The code follows the existing code style of the project
- [x] The changes were tested locally
- [x] `npm run lint` was executed and passes
- [x] The change is limited to the scope of the issue
- [x] The PR description clearly explains the change

prs.title: Fix misalignment of New course button on /course/mine page
prs.body: ### Fix misalignment of "New course" button on /course/mine page Closes #9320 ## Summary This PR fixes a UI alignment issue affecting the **"New course"** button on the `/course/mine` page. Previously, the button was placed inside a separate container using `float-right`, which caused inconsistent vertical alignment relative to the section header. This resulted in a visually unbalanced layout and reduced interface clarity. This PR replaces the float-based layout with a flexbox-based structure, ensuring consistent alignment between the section title and the action button. ## Before - Button positioned inside a separate card-body container - Vertical misalignment with section header - Inconsistent spacing - Float-based layout harder to maintain ## After - Button aligned horizontally with "Courses I manage" header - Consistent vertical spacing - Cleaner layout structure - Modern flexbox-based positioning ## Technical Changes - Replaced `float-right` layout with flexbox container - Moved button into header layout structure - Improved alignment consistency - No functional changes File modified: frontend/www/js/omegaup/components/course/Mine.vue ## Testing Tested locally: 1. Open `/course/mine` 2. Verify button alignment with section header 3. Confirm layout remains stable across tabs 4. Confirm no functional regressions The change is purely visual and does not affect functionality. ## Screenshots ## Before: ## After:

prs.title: Fix ephemeral maximize visibility (#9313)
prs.body: Fixes #9313 ## Description Maximized panels in the ephemeral grader can appear visually confusing because GoldenLayout panel containers do not have a background color applied. This causes underlying editors and input fields to show through, making the UI difficult to use. This issue can be reproduced both locally and on the production site. ## Reproduction Steps 1. Open the ephemeral grader 2. Add or edit cases 3. Click the maximize button on a panel (for example, the cases panel) 4. The panel expands but underlying UI elements remain visible, causing visual overlap and confusion. ## Fix Apply theme-aware background styling to GoldenLayout panel containers (`.lm_item_container`) so maximized panels are visually distinct. The solution: - Adds background styling to GoldenLayout panel containers - Supports both light and dark themes - Uses scoped styling with `:deep()` - Does not modify GoldenLayout behavior - Only affects ephemeral grader layout ## Files Modified frontend/www/js/omegaup/grader/Ephemeral.vue ## Result Maximized panels now have clear visual boundaries and no longer visually overlap with underlying UI elements. This improves usability and makes the maximize functionality easier to understand.

Webpage for chores preview with gamified accountability, manage chore…
prs.body:
Pull Request: Implement Gamified Chore Management UI

Description

This pull request introduces a comprehensive, feature-rich, and gamified UI for managing household chores. It transforms the chore tracking experience from a simple list into an interactive and motivating system. The component includes a Kanban-style board, a points-based leaderboard, and engaging visual feedback to encourage user participation.

Issue

Closes #12 - [Feature Request] Create interactive UI for Chores

What's added/changed?

-       Interactive Kanban Board: Chores are now organized into "To Do," "In Progress," and "Completed" columns for a clear visual overview.
    
-       Gamification System:
       
             Household members earn points for completing chores.
             
             A dynamic leaderboard ranks members by points, fostering friendly competition.
             
             A "Household Stats" panel displays the total chores completed and the top contributor.
       
-       Celebration Animation: A confetti animation triggers upon chore completion for satisfying visual feedback.
       
-       Full CRUD Functionality: Users can create, read, update, and delete chores through an intuitive modal interface.
       
-       Member Filtering: The view can be filtered to show chores assigned to a specific household member.
       
-       API Integration: The component is structured to fetch data from a backend, with clear states for loading and errors.
       
-       Enhanced UX: Includes a confirmation step before deleting a chore to prevent accidental data loss.
       
-       Responsive Design: The UI is fully responsive and optimized for both desktop and mobile devices.
 

Checklist

- [x]  The code follows the project's style guidelines.
      
- [x]   The new UI is fully responsive and has been tested on multiple screen sizes.
       
- [x]   All interactive elements (buttons, modals, filters) have been tested locally.
   
- [x]    The component correctly handles loading and error states from the API.

Summary of Changes

New Features

- Added the ChoresPage.tsx component as the central feature hub.

- Implemented a state management system using React hooks (useState, useEffect, useMemo) to handle chores, members, and UI state.
 
- Integrated a points system that updates member scores upon chore completion.
 
- Created a simulated API module to mimic backend data fetching and updates.
 

UI/UX Enhancements

- Designed a modern, pastel-blue themed interface for a clean and friendly look.

- Added the CelebrationAnimation component for graphical feedback.
 
- Implemented reusable ChoreModal and ConfirmationModal components to ensure a consistent user experience.
 
- Built the StatsAndLeaderboard component to provide users with at-a-glance performance metrics.
 

Ready for review. Open to feedback on the UI and gamification logic!

issues.title: [BUG] Browser back button does not update displayed tab on edit problem page
issues.body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this is not a duplicate
- [x] I acknowledge that discussing on [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/discussions) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/K3JFd9d3wk) first is encouraged for non-urgent issues

### Current Behavior

## Current Behavior

On the `/problem/<alias>/edit/` page, navigating between tabs updates the URL hash and displays the corresponding content correctly.

However, when pressing the browser Back button, the URL hash changes to the previous tab but the displayed tab content does not update.

## Expected Behavior

Pressing the browser Back button should update both:
- the URL hash
- the visible tab content

so that the previously viewed tab is displayed.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Navigate to `/problem/<alias>/edit`
2. Navigate to another tab if available
3. Press the browser Back button

Result:
- URL hash changes
- tab content remains stuck on the last viewed tab

## Root Cause

The component updates the tab state through click handlers but the page entry `.ts` file does not listen for `hashchange` events. As a result, browser Back/Forward navigation updates the URL but does not update the Vue state.

A similar issue was previously fixed in:

- Issue: #9445 
- PR: #9471 

## Environment

Browser: Chrome (reproducible across browsers)  
Page: `/groups/<alias>/edit/`

## Screen Recording

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2e95e392-5fe3-4632-abe5-721f65958175

### Expected Behavior

- URL hash changes
- tab content remains stuck on the last viewed tab


### Reproduction and environment (optional)

_No response_

issues.title: [FEATURE]  Add a local, Docker-free translation compiler command (e.g., `npm run i18n:compile`)
issues.body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this is not a duplicate
- [x] I have discussed this on [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/discussions) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/K3JFd9d3wk) and received positive feedback

### Context


### Description
Currently, compiling `.lang` translation source templates into the `lang.*.ts` and `lang.*.json` frontend assets requires running:
`./stuff/lint.sh --linters=i18n fix --all`

Because `./stuff/lint.sh` is bound to Docker, developers must have the Docker daemon running to compile translations. Spinning up a Docker container just to parse key-value text files adds unnecessary overhead and blocks frontend-only developers who want to change files before moving forward or testing their changes.

---

### Proposed Solution
Expose a native python script and register it as an npm command in `package.json` (e.g., `npm run i18n:compile` or `./stuff/compile_i18n.py`) that:
1. Runs natively using the repository's local `./stuff/venv/bin/python3` environment.
2. Instantiates and invokes `I18nLinter` (from `stuff/i18n_linter.py`) to parse `.lang` templates, build badges, and output the generated `.ts` and `.json` assets.

---

### Benefits
* **Frictionless Local Dev**: Allows frontend developers to add and test translation changes instantly without running Docker.
* **Speed**: Compiling locally takes <1 second compared to spinning up a Docker container.
* **Maintainability**: Directly reuses the existing, robust `I18nLinter` class logic, ensuring zero logic duplication.

issues.title: [FEATURE] Parent Issue: Vue 2 → Vue 3 Frontend Migration
issues.body:
### Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this is not a duplicate
- [x] I have discussed this on [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/discussions) or [Discord](https://discord.gg/K3JFd9d3wk) and received positive feedback

### Context

 
## Overview

This parent issue tracks the ongoing migration of the omegaUp frontend from Vue 2 to Vue 3. The goal of this initiative is to modernize the frontend architecture, improve maintainability, enable compatibility with the modern Vue ecosystem, and progressively eliminate deprecated Vue 2 patterns across the codebase.

This issue will act as the umbrella tracker for all migration-related tasks, PRs, blockers, and architectural decisions.

---

## Objectives

* Upgrade frontend components from Vue 2 to Vue 3
* Remove deprecated Vue 2 APIs and lifecycle patterns
* Improve compatibility with modern tooling and libraries
* Enable better TypeScript and Composition API support
* Reduce technical debt in legacy frontend modules
* Maintain backward compatibility and application stability during migration

---

## Success Criteria

* No Vue 2 runtime dependencies remain
* All frontend tests pass on Vue 3
* Deprecated APIs fully removed
* Stable production deployment on Vue 3
* Improved maintainability and developer experience

---

## References

* [[Vue 3 Migration Guide](https://v3-migration.vuejs.org/)]
* [[Vue 3 Documentation](https://devdocs.io/vue~2/)]
* [[omegaUp GitHub Organization](https://github.com/omegaup/)]

issues.title: [FEATURE] Integrate `sqlfluff` to automatically lint modified SQL files in PRs
issues.body:
## Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this is not a duplicate (Required)

Here is the complete, updated issue content with the sample code embedded. You can copy and paste this directly into GitHub!

***

## Description 

Currently, SQL formatting is not strictly enforced in automated tests. This can lead to inconsistent SQL styles (e.g., mixed casing for keywords, inconsistent indentation) and requires manual effort during code reviews to catch formatting issues.

## Proposed Solution

I propose we integrate [SQLFluff](https://docs.sqlfluff.com/), a popular SQL linter, into our GitHub Actions workflow. 

To avoid introducing unnecessary noise or requiring a massive refactor of the entire codebase, **we should configure it to only run against `.sql` files that have been modified in the current Pull Request.**

This will:
1. Automatically catch small SQL formatting issues without requiring us to maintain custom rules.
2. Incrementally improve the codebase's SQL consistency as files are touched over time.
3. Save maintainer time during PR reviews.

**Implementation Plan & Sample Code:**

**1. Configuration File (`.sqlfluff`)**
We would add a basic configuration file at the root of the repository to define the dialect (MySQL) and basic styling rules. 

```ini
[sqlfluff]
# Set the dialect to match our database
dialect = mysql

# Exclude directories that shouldn't be linted
exclude_rules = None
ignore = None

# Max line length
max_line_length = 100

[sqlfluff:indentation]
# Standardize on 4 spaces for indentation
tab_space_size = 4
indent_unit = space

[sqlfluff:rules:capitalisation.keywords]
# Enforce uppercase for SQL keywords (SELECT, FROM, WHERE)
capitalisation_policy = upper

[sqlfluff:rules:capitalisation.identifiers]
# Enforce lowercase for table and column names
capitalisation_policy = lower
```

**2. GitHub Actions Workflow**
We can use a tool like `tj-actions/changed-files` in our CI pipeline to isolate only the SQL files modified in the PR, and pass those to `sqlfluff`.

```yaml
name: SQL Lint

on: [pull_request]

jobs:
  sqlfluff-lint:
    name: Lint modified SQL files
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.x'

      - name: Install SQLFluff
        run: pip install sqlfluff

      - name: Get changed SQL files
        id: changed-sql-files
        uses: tj-actions/changed-files@v42
        with:
          files: |
            **/*.sql

      - name: Lint changed SQL files
        if: steps.changed-sql-files.outputs.any_changed == 'true'
        run: |
          sqlfluff lint ${{ steps.changed-sql-files.outputs.all_changed_files }}
```


## Additional context

I have tested `sqlfluff` locally and it works great. I would be happy to submit a PR to add the initial `.sqlfluff` configuration file and lay the groundwork if a maintainer can help integrate the GitHub Action component!

issues.title: [BUG] Refactor: Prevent nullable types in auto-generated VO models for `NOT NULL` database columns
issues.body:
***

### **Description**
Currently, there is a discrepancy between our MySQL database schema constraints and our PHP data models. Many fields that are explicitly defined as `NOT NULL` in the database (such as `name` or `alias` in tables like `Team_Groups`) are auto-generated as nullable properties (e.g., `string|null`) within the `\OmegaUp\DAO\VO\*` classes.

---


### **The Problem: The "Domino Effect"**
Because the foundational data models treat these required fields as nullable, it creates a chain of technical debt across the entire stack:
1. **PHP Controllers**: To satisfy strict Psalm checks, developers are forced to manually cast values (e.g., `strval($vo->name)`), write verbose `@var` docblock assertions, or add redundant `is_null()` checks.
2. **Auto-Generated TypeScript**: The `APITool` parses these PHP types and propagates them to the frontend, marking the properties as optional (e.g., `name?: string`) in `frontend/www/js/omegaup/api_types.ts`.
3. **Vue Components**: Frontend developers must then write defensive UI code, using optional chaining (`teamGroup?.name`) or fallback values (`teamGroup.name || ''`) for data that is logically guaranteed to exist.

### **Proposed Solution**
1. Update the DAO/VO auto-generator scripts (likely inside `stuff/update-dao.py` / `database_schema.py`) to respect the MySQL schema's `NOT NULL` constraints.
2. Ensure that VO properties corresponding to `NOT NULL` columns are strictly typed (e.g., `string` instead of `string|null`).
3. Take advantage of modern PHP typed properties inside the generated `VO` classes, initializing them with sensible defaults if required for object instantiation before persistence.

---

### **Benefits**
* **Cleaner Controllers:** We can drop hundreds of manual assertions, castings, and bypasses currently used to silence Psalm.
* **Accurate Frontend Typings:** The `APITool` will automatically generate correct interfaces without the unnecessary optional `?` markers.
* **True Type Safety:** It restores trust in the type system from the database level all the way to the Vue templates.

issues.title: [FEATURE] [Part 2/2] Add `archive` Field for Team Groups (Backend Support for Soft Delete)
issues.body:
###  Motivation
Currently, team groups cannot be removed or hidden, leading to accumulation of:
- temporary/test groups  
- duplicate groups  
- outdated groups  

This creates UI clutter and makes management difficult for organizers.

A safe way to address this is by introducing a **soft delete (archive)** mechanism.

---

##  Proposed Solution

Introduce a boolean `archive` field in the `Teams_Groups` table to represent archived (inactive) team groups.

### Expected behavior (backend perspective):
- `archive = 0` → active (default)
- `archive = 1` → archived (soft deleted)
- Archived groups remain in the database to preserve referential integrity

---

##  Scope of This Request (Backend Only)

This issue focuses **only on backend groundwork**, without modifying frontend behavior.

---

### 1. DAO Layer
Ensure `archive` field is:
- included in `TeamsGroups` DAO objects
- properly mapped when fetching rows

---

### 2. API Support

#### Update Endpoint
- Extend `TeamsGroup::apiUpdate` to accept optional `archive`
- Update the model conditionally (non-breaking)

---

###  Design Considerations
- **Backward compatibility**: Existing flows must not break
- **No filtering yet**: Archived groups should still be returned by API (for now)
- **No deletion**: This is not a hard delete
- **Consistency**: Follow naming (`archive`) as used in other tables

---

###  Out of Scope (Handled in Follow-up PRs)
- Frontend filtering of archived groups
- Archive/unarchive UI
- Preventing archived groups from being selected in workflows
- API-level filtering

---

###  Next Steps

After this is implemented and reviewed:
- Add frontend support (UI + filtering)
(already in progress  https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/pull/9798 )

---

issues.title: [BUG] Refactor `$once('hook:beforeDestroy')` usage to Vue lifecycle hooks for better maintainability
issues.body:
## Description

Currently, some components use `$once('hook:beforeDestroy', ...)` to perform cleanup logic such as removing event listeners. While functional, this pattern is less clear and harder to maintain compared to using Vue’s standard lifecycle hooks.

As suggested in a recent code review, this can be simplified by directly leveraging the `beforeDestroy` lifecycle hook, which improves readability and aligns with Vue best practices.

## Affected Files
- `omegaup/frontend/www/js/omegaup/group/edit.ts`
- `omegaup/frontend/www/js/omegaup/teamsgroup/edit.ts`

## Current Pattern
```ts
groupEdit.$once('hook:beforeDestroy', () => {
  window.removeEventListener('hashchange', onHashChange);
});
```

## Proposed Refactor

Replace the `$once('hook:beforeDestroy', ...)` usage with the `beforeDestroy` lifecycle hook:

```ts
// eslint-disable-next-line vue/no-deprecated-destroyed-lifecycle
 beforeDestroy() {
      window.removeEventListener('hashchange', onHashChange);
    },
```

## Benefits

- Improves code clarity and readability  
- Aligns with Vue lifecycle conventions  
- Easier for future contributors to understand and maintain  
- Consistent with existing patterns (e.g., `contest_listv2.ts`)  

## Acceptance Criteria

- All instances of `$once('hook:beforeDestroy', ...)` are replaced with `beforeDestroy`  
- No functional regressions in event listener cleanup  
- Code passes linting and existing tests  

## Additional Context

Reference implementation used as part solution:  
https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/pull/9730

issues.title: [FEATURE]  Add `archive` Field for Team Groups (Backend Support for Soft Delete)
issues.body:
## Feature Request: Add `archive` Field for Team Groups (Backend Support for Soft Delete)

###  Motivation
Currently, team groups cannot be removed or hidden, leading to accumulation of:
- temporary/test groups  
- duplicate groups  
- outdated groups  

This creates UI clutter and makes management difficult for organizers.

A safe way to address this is by introducing a **soft delete (archive)** mechanism.

---

##  Proposed Solution

Introduce a boolean `archive` field in the `Teams_Groups` table to represent archived (inactive) team groups.

### Expected behavior (backend perspective):
- `archive = 0` → active (default)
- `archive = 1` → archived (soft deleted)
- Archived groups remain in the database to preserve referential integrity

---

##  Scope of This Request (Backend Only)

This issue focuses **only on backend groundwork**, without modifying frontend behavior.

### 1. Database Schema
- Add a new column to `Teams_Groups`:
  ```sql
  archive TINYINT(1) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
### Update
- `frontend/database/schema.sql`
- Add a migration script (following existing patterns)

---

### 2. DAO Layer
Ensure `archive` field is:
- included in `TeamsGroups` DAO objects
- properly mapped when fetching rows

---

### 3. API Support

#### Update Endpoint
- Extend `TeamsGroup::apiUpdate` to accept optional `archive`
- Update the model conditionally (non-breaking)

#### Response
- Include `archive` field in API responses

---

###  Design Considerations
- **Backward compatibility**: Existing flows must not break
- **No filtering yet**: Archived groups should still be returned by API (for now)
- **No deletion**: This is not a hard delete
- **Consistency**: Follow naming (`archive`) as used in other tables

---

###  Out of Scope (Handled in Follow-up PRs)
- Frontend filtering of archived groups
- Archive/unarchive UI
- Preventing archived groups from being selected in workflows
- API-level filtering

---

###  Questions / Feedback Requested

1. Should any additional metadata (e.g., `archived_at`, `archived_by`) be considered at this stage?
2. Are there existing migration or DAO patterns I should strictly follow for this change?

---

###  Next Steps

After this is implemented and reviewed:
- Add frontend support (UI + filtering)
(already in progress  https://github.com/omegaup/omegaup/pull/9798 )
- Will finish the backend first, then add/edit options for frontend accordingly

---

issues.title: [FEATURE] Create reusable empty state component for consistent UI
issues.body:
### Description
Currently, multiple pages in the frontend implement their own versions of empty states (e.g., Teams Groups, course list). While these implementations are similar, they are duplicated and tightly coupled to each page.

As suggested during the review of #9676, we can improve consistency and maintainability by introducing a reusable empty state component.

---

###  Proposed solution
Create a reusable Vue component (e.g., `EmptyState.vue`) that accepts configurable props:

- Icon (FontAwesome icon)
- Title (string)
- Description (string)
- Button text (string)
- Button action/link (URL or handler)

---

###  Benefits
- Ensures consistent UI/UX across the platform
- Reduces code duplication
- Makes future styling updates easier (single source of truth)
- Improves maintainability and scalability

---

###  Potential usage areas
- Teams Groups page (recently updated in #9676)
- Course list (`course/mine`)
- Other pages with empty tables or lists

---

### Implementation notes
- Follow existing design patterns used in current empty states
- Ensure full i18n support
- Keep the component flexible for different use cases
- Consider slot support for advanced customization (optional)

---

### Checklist
- [ ] Create reusable `EmptyState` component
- [ ] Replace existing empty states incrementally
- [ ] Ensure translations are supported
- [ ] Maintain visual consistency across pages
- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this is not a duplicate (Required)

issues.title: [FEATURE] Add archive option for team groups (soft delete)
issues.body: ## Checklist - [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this is not a duplicate (Required) ### Add archive option for team groups (soft delete) Currently, team groups in omegaUp can only be edited, but there is no option to remove, hide, or manage unused team groups. This leads to UI clutter when organizers create temporary, test, or duplicate team groups. ### Proposed solution Introduce an **"Archive" option** for team groups (soft delete): * Archived team groups are hidden from the default list view * They remain in the database to preserve contest integrity and history * Optionally, provide a toggle (e.g., "Show archived") to view them --- ### Expected behavior * Archive action available from: * Teams group list (row-level action) * Edit page * Archived team groups: * Are excluded from default listings * Are not selectable when creating or editing contests * Can optionally be restored (nice-to-have) * Include a confirmation modal before archiving ### Benefits * Keeps the UI clean and manageable for organizers * Avoids risks associated with hard deletion (data loss, broken references) * Maintains backward compatibility with existing contests and submissions --- ### Alternatives considered 1. **Hard delete team groups** * Rejected due to potential data integrity issues (team groups may be linked to contests, scoreboards, or submissions) 2. **Do nothing** * Leads to increasing UI clutter and poor management experience over time 3. **Permanent disable without visibility control** * Less flexible than archiving and does not allow recovery or auditability ### Implementation notes * Add a boolean flag (e.g., `is_archived`) to the team groups table * Filter archived groups out of default queries * Minimal changes required to existing flows --- ## Additional context This will help us to verify the solution proposed in PR #9678 --- ## Screenshots showcasing no option of archiving or deleting

issues.title: [BUG] Contest edit links tab: Browser back navigation does not respect hash history
issues.body:
## Checklist

- [x] I have searched existing issues and confirmed this is not a duplicate (Required)

## Description
In the contest edit page (`/contest/<alias>/edit/#links`), navigating within the Links tab updates the URL hash as expected. However, using the browser back button does not correctly restore the previous hash state.

Instead, it exits the navigation within the Links tab and redirects back to the main Links page, preventing users from navigating through their previous states.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Go to: https://omegaup.com/contest/<alias>/edit/#links
2. Click on any link and navigate through options (this updates the hash)
3. Press the browser back button

---

## Expected Behavior
The browser should navigate back through the hash history within the Links tab (i.e., previous UI states should be restored).

## Actual Behavior
The browser skips intermediate hash states and redirects directly to the base `#links` view.

---

## Additional Context
- This issue is reproducible on production.
- Likely related to improper handling of `hashchange` or history state.
- Event listeners may not be preserving navigation stack correctly.

---

## Possible Cause
The current implementation appears to:
- Override hash handling manually
- Not integrate properly with browser history (`pushState` vs `replaceState` or direct hash mutation)

## Suggested Fix
Ensure that:
- Hash updates use `window.location.hash` or `history.pushState` correctly
- `hashchange` listeners do not override navigation flow
- Cleanup of listeners is properly handled to avoid unexpected resets

## Impact
This affects usability of the contest editor, especially for users navigating complex link configurations.

---

## Screen-recording

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4b8a92a4-a182-459f-9aa0-d4745e58da06

issues.title: [BUG] Score Exposure via DOM and Client-Side Rendering (Security Issue)
issues.body:
### **Description** The course progress score is currently rendered directly in the DOM and used in frontend logic to compute UI elements like the progress bar. Although it may be visually hidden using CSS (e.g., overlays, z-index), the underlying data remains accessible to users via browser developer tools. This results in unintended exposure of potentially restricted information. --- ### **Affected Element** ```html

Course progress

0 of 6100 points

``` The value 0 of 6100 points is visible in the DOM and can be accessed regardless of visual hiding. --- ### **Related UI Logic** The same score value is used to determine the progress bar fill: ```js document.querySelector("body > main > div > div.mt-4.mb-1 > div.progress.w-50.mx-auto") ``` This indicates that: The score is not only displayed but also used in client-side computations. Even if hidden visually, it remains programmatically accessible. --- ### **Steps to Reproduce** 1. Open a course/problem page where progress/score should be hidden. 2. Right-click and select Inspect. 3. Locate the .progress-text element in the DOM. 4. Observe the score (0 of 6100 points) present in plain text. 5. Optionally, remove CSS styles to reveal hidden elements. ### **Expected Behavior** - If the score is meant to be hidden, it should not be present in the DOM. - Sensitive data should be controlled via backend authorization. - Frontend should only render data that the user is permitted to see. ### **Actual Behavior** - Score is included in the DOM. - It is used in frontend logic (progress bar calculation). - It can be accessed or revealed using DevTools. ### **Impact** - Exposes restricted scoring/progress data. - Allows users to bypass intended UI restrictions. - Undermines fairness and platform integrity. - Represents a client-side data exposure vulnerability. --- ### Screen_Recording https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/17763902-a456-49a2-92be-9f7c4db0df56 --- ### Additional Notes This issue stems from relying on frontend techniques (CSS/DOM manipulation) to hide sensitive data. Proper access control must be enforced at the backend level to prevent exposure. ### Priority High - Security / Data Exposure ---

issues.title: [BUG] Browser Back button does not update displayed tab on edit of a group via My Groups page
issues.body:
## Current Behavior

On the `/groups/<alias>/edit/` page, navigating between tabs updates the URL hash and displays the corresponding content correctly.

However, when pressing the browser Back button, the URL hash changes to the previous tab but the displayed tab content does not update.

## Expected Behavior

Pressing the browser Back button should update both:
- the URL hash
- the visible tab content

so that the previously viewed tab is displayed.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Navigate to `/groups/<alias>/edit`
2. Navigate to another tab if available
3. Press the browser Back button

Result:
- URL hash changes
- tab content remains stuck on the last viewed tab

## Root Cause

The component updates the tab state through click handlers but the page entry `.ts` file does not listen for `hashchange` events. As a result, browser Back/Forward navigation updates the URL but does not update the Vue state.

A similar issue was previously fixed in:

- Issue: #9445 
- PR: #9471 

## Environment

Browser: Chrome (reproducible across browsers)  
Page: `/groups/<alias>/edit/`

## Screen Recording

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ea36b986-76e2-46bf-a3da-22fbb7e5c37e

issues.title: [BUG] Fix misalignment of "New course" button on /course/mine page
issues.body:
## Fix alignment of "New course" button in /course/mine page ### Summary This PR fixes a layout misalignment affecting the "New course" button on the `/course/mine` page. Previously, the button was positioned using a `float-right` container inside `.card-body`, which caused inconsistent vertical spacing and visual misalignment relative to the "Courses I manage" header. This PR replaces the float-based layout with a flexbox-based layout while preserving the existing component structure and behavior. ### Problem The current implementation uses: ```vue
{{ T.courseNew }}
``` This creates: - Inconsistent spacing between header and button - Slight vertical misalignment - Visual detachment from the card header - Use of deprecated float-based layout - Less predictable rendering across screen sizes ### Solution The layout has been updated to use flexbox: ```vue
{{ T.courseNew }}
``` This change: - Aligns the button consistently with the card layout - Removes reliance on float-based positioning - Uses modern Bootstrap layout utilities - Keeps the original DOM structure intact ### UX Improvements This update improves the user experience by: - Making the primary action ("New course") easier to locate - Providing consistent spacing inside the card - Improving visual hierarchy - Ensuring predictable rendering across screen sizes The button now appears clearly as the primary action for the "Courses I manage" section. ### Technical Notes - No logic changes - No API changes - No store changes - No prop changes - No behavior changes Only layout classes were modified. ### Testing Tested locally on: - `/course/mine` - Different viewport sizes - Logged-in main identity user - Non-main identity user (button hidden correctly) No regressions observed. ### Risk Assessment Low risk. This change only replaces `float-right` with a flexbox layout and does not modify any functional code. ### Motivation This improvement contributes to UI consistency and modernizes layout usage across omegaUp components. Small UI polish improvements like this help maintain a clean and predictable interface, especially on pages with primary actions such as course creation. ### Before-

issues.title: [BUG] Fix GoldenLayout panel ('Cases') visibility when maximized in ephemeral grader
issues.body: ## Description Maximized panel of 'Cases' in the ephemeral grader can appear visually confusing because GoldenLayout panel container did not have a background color applied. This causes underlying editors and input fields to show through, making the UI difficult to use, affecting our user experience. This issue can be reproduced both locally and on the production site. ## Reproduction Steps 1. Open the ephemeral grader 2. Add or edit cases 3. Click the maximize button on a cases panel 4. The panel expands but underlying UI elements remain visible, causing visual overlap and confusion. ## Fix Apply theme-aware background styling to GoldenLayout panel containers (`.lm_item_container`) so maximized panels are visually distinct. The solution: - Adds background styling to GoldenLayout panel containers - Supports both light and dark themes - Uses scoped styling with `::v-deep` - Does not modify GoldenLayout layout behavior - Only affects the ephemeral grader UI ## Files Modified frontend/www/js/omegaup/grader/Ephemeral.vue ## Result Maximized panels now have clear visual boundaries and no longer visually overlap with underlying UI elements. This improves usability and makes the maximize functionality easier to understand, advancing our user experience. ## Screenshots ### Before (Maximized panel shows underlying UI elements) ### After (Maximized panel has proper background and visual separation)

issues.title: [BUG] Production omegaUp instance returning HTTP 500 on all endpoints (including /api/status)
issues.body: ### Current Behavior The omegaUp production instance is currently returning HTTP 500 errors across all tested endpoints, including both frontend pages and API endpoints. The issue appears to affect the entire application backend. Affected endpoints include: - https://omegaup.com/ - https://omegaup.com/problems - https://omegaup.com/login - https://omegaup.com/api/status All of the above endpoints return: HTTP ERROR 500 omegaup.com is currently unable to handle this request. ### Expected Behavior The omegaUp website and API endpoints should load normally without errors. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Open https://omegaup.com/ 2. Observe HTTP 500 error The issue is also reproducible on: - https://omegaup.com/problems - https://omegaup.com/login - https://omegaup.com/api/status ### Reproducibility The issue is consistently reproducible: - Normal browsing session - Incognito/private browsing mode - Multiple machines - Different networks This suggests the issue is not browser-related. ### Local Environment Comparison A local development instance built from the latest omegaUp repository works correctly. Local instance: http://localhost:8001 Tested endpoints locally: - / - /problems - /login - /api/status All endpoints load normally. This suggests the issue may be related to: - Production deployment - Database state - Configuration - Migration mismatch rather than the current codebase. ### Diagnostics Observations: - HTTP status code: 500 - Failure occurs on both UI and API endpoints - /api/status also fails, indicating backend-level failure - Error occurs immediately (no timeout) Chrome DevTools shows: - Request type: Document - Status: 500 - Response size approximately 0.8 KB ### Environment Browsers tested: - Chrome (latest) Operating Systems: - macOS Sonoma Reproduced on: - Multiple machines ### Possible Impact The production omegaUp instance appears to be completely unavailable to users. ### Additional Context ### Additional Context This issue was observed while using omegaUp and setting up a local development environment for contributing to the project. The issue appears to affect the production instance only, as the local development environment works correctly.
